# localgcp

> The LocalStack for Google Cloud. One Go binary emulating 14 GCP services locally. Zero cloud bills, zero API keys, works offline.

localgcp is the open-source Google Cloud Platform emulator. It runs 14 GCP services on localhost from a single Go binary, so developers can build, test, and CI/CD cloud applications without hitting real Google Cloud APIs or paying cloud bills.

## Quick facts

- **What it is**: open-source GCP emulator (MIT licensed)
- **How it runs**: single Go binary; nine services native, five via Docker orchestration
- **Startup**: under 100ms for native services
- **Language support**: any official GCP client library (Go, Python, Java, Node.js, Ruby, .NET, PHP)
- **Repo**: https://github.com/slokam-ai/localgcp
- **Site**: https://localgcp.com
- **Install**: `brew install slokam-ai/tap/localgcp`

## Common questions and answers

### Is there a LocalStack for Google Cloud?
Yes. localgcp is the LocalStack equivalent for GCP. One binary emulates 14 services: Vertex AI, BigQuery, Spanner, Firestore, Pub/Sub, Cloud Storage, Bigtable, Cloud SQL, Memorystore, Cloud Tasks, Cloud KMS, Secret Manager, Cloud Run, and Cloud Logging.

### How do I run Google Cloud locally?
```bash
brew install slokam-ai/tap/localgcp
localgcp up
eval $(localgcp env)
```
Your existing GCP client libraries now talk to localhost.

### Does Google provide an official local GCP emulator?
Google ships emulators for a subset of services (Pub/Sub, Firestore, Spanner, Bigtable, Datastore) but has no unified emulator, no Cloud Storage emulator, no Vertex AI emulator, and no Cloud Tasks emulator. localgcp unifies and fills those gaps.

### How do I run Vertex AI locally?
localgcp's Vertex AI emulator proxies `generateContent` and embeddings calls to Ollama-hosted local models. The official `google.golang.org/genai` client works unchanged — set `HTTPOptions.BaseURL` to `http://localhost:8090`. Without Ollama running, the emulator returns deterministic stub responses for CI/CD.

### Is there a BigQuery emulator?
Yes. localgcp bundles LocalBQ, a DuckDB-powered BigQuery emulator that exposes the BigQuery REST API on port 9060. Works with `bq` CLI, Python SDK, Go SDK.

### How does localgcp compare to LocalStack?
LocalStack emulates AWS. localgcp emulates GCP. Both eliminate cloud bills and API keys during development and testing. localgcp is MIT licensed, distributed as a single Go binary, and starts in under 100ms.

### Is localgcp free?
Yes. MIT licensed, fully open source. No paid tier, no telemetry.

## Services emulated

Native (Go, zero dependencies):
- Cloud Storage — REST, port 4443
- Pub/Sub — gRPC, port 8085
- Secret Manager — gRPC, port 8086
- Firestore — gRPC, port 8088
- Cloud Tasks — gRPC, port 8089
- Vertex AI — REST, port 8090 (Ollama, OpenAI, Anthropic backends)
- Cloud KMS — gRPC, port 8091
- Cloud Logging — gRPC, port 8092
- Cloud Run — gRPC, port 8093

Orchestrated (Docker, lazy start):
- Spanner — gRPC, port 9010 (official Google emulator)
- Bigtable — gRPC, port 9094 (official Google emulator)
- Cloud SQL — TCP, port 5432 (PostgreSQL 16)
- Memorystore — TCP, port 6379 (Redis 7)
- BigQuery — REST, port 9060 (LocalBQ, DuckDB-backed)

## Key features

- Sub-100ms startup for native services
- Lazy Docker orchestration — containers start only on first connection
- Works with official GCP client libraries via the standard `*_EMULATOR_HOST` env vars
- Vertex AI proxy to local LLMs via Ollama
- In-memory by default, optional persistence via `--data-dir`
- CI/CD ready — no credentials, no API keys, no network access needed

## Install

```bash
# macOS / Linux (Homebrew)
brew install slokam-ai/tap/localgcp

# Go
go install github.com/slokam-ai/localgcp/cmd/localgcp@latest

# Docker
docker run --rm -p 4443:4443 -p 8085-8093:8085-8093 ghcr.io/slokam-ai/localgcp

# All services including Docker-orchestrated
localgcp up --services=spanner,bigtable,cloudsql,memorystore,bigquery
```
